---
title: "Pigmentation Genetics in Archaic Hominins: Evaluating DNA phenotyping in Neanderthal and Denisovan genomes"
author: "Lily Heald"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: true  
    toc_depth: 2 
    code_folding: hide  
site: workflowr::wflow_site
---

# Abstract

The question of what our archaic hominin ancestors may have looked like has captured the attention of scientists and popular culture. Inferring phenotypes from DNA is a challenging statistical problem, further complicated by inference in unobservable archaic populations. Previous research has sequenced pigmentation related variants in ancient and archaic samples, but this work remains spread across single locus studies and phenotype prediction attempts rather than directly consolidated into a systematic archaic-focused resource. Here, I attempt to evaluate the feasibility of phenotype prediction in 14 archaic Neanderthal and Denisovan individuals through the analysis of depth and missingness in available genomes, and compare available pigmentation-related SNPs in these archaic populations to diverse modern human genomes obtained from the Simons Genome Diversity Project. PCAs were constructed using the modern human genomes, and archaic samples were then projected onto the PC space. Sequencing depth of the archaic samples was calculated using SAMtools. Analyses of depth and missingness were performed as a mixed effects logistic regression of missingness and two simulations of the confidence in the consensus base call conferred by sequence depth. There is significant variation in coverage and depth across archaic samples. Missingness, for which age and structural influences are significant predictors, is also non-random. Furthermore, untreated ancient DNA is significantly affected by low coverage. Archaic samples cluster near Eurasians in a genome-wide SNP panel, but with Africans in a pigmentation panel. This emphasizes the importance of caution and conservative statistical methods when attempting to make sweeping species-wide phenotypic predictions from archaic DNA.

# Key Results
Background on the samples analyzed is available on the **Introduction** analysis page:
- [View UV map and aDNA sites](introduction.html)

Population genetics analyses are available on the **Analysis** page:
- [View genetic PCAs](analysis.html)

# Data Sources
- NASA Earth Observations: [AURA_UVI_CLIM_M](https://neo.gsfc.nasa.gov/view.php?datasetId=AURA_UVI_CLIM_M)
- NHGRI-EBI Catalog of GWAS associated pigmentation variants: [NHGRI-EBI](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/gwas/efotraits/OBA_VT0002095)
- Simons Genome Diversity Project: [SGDP](https://www.simonsfoundation.org/simons-genome-diversity-project/)
- Max-Plank Institute neanderthal genomes: [MPI-EVA](http://ftp.eva.mpg.de/neandertal/)
